description: Fix 13 genuine defects found during a comprehensive platform audit: 2 security gaps, 3 behavior bugs, 3 dead code items, 2 missing validations, 2 code quality duplications, and 1 inconsistency across mcp-server, browser-extension, and slack plugin.

userStories.title: Remove dead code: getNextRequestId unused _state parameter and deprecated nextRequestId field
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dead code removed from state.ts so that the codebase is clean and unconfusing.
acceptanceCriteria: getNextRequestId in state.ts takes zero parameters and returns string
All call sites updated from getNextRequestId(state) to getNextRequestId()
nextRequestId field removed from ServerState interface
If STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION needs bumping, it is bumped
Any hot-reload patching loop in index.ts that references nextRequestId is updated
userStories.notes: state.ts:177 — getNextRequestId takes _state: ServerState but only calls crypto.randomUUID(). Remove the parameter. Grep for getNextRequestId(state) to find all call sites — currently in extension-protocol.ts and browser-tools/reload-extension.ts. Also remove the deprecated nextRequestId field from the ServerState interface at lines 111-115. Check index.ts for the hot-reload patching loop that iterates over createState() defaults — it may reference nextRequestId. Bump STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION at line 149 if removing the field changes schema compat (though since it's optional and unused, bumping may not be needed — use your judgment).

userStories.title: Add npmPlugins to FALLBACK_CONFIG for consistency
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all config creation paths to produce the same shape so that callers don't need null coalescing for npmPlugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Both fallback paths (line 103 and line 139) now produce configs with npmPlugins: []
userStories.notes: config.ts:70-74 — FALLBACK_CONFIG is { plugins: [], tools: {}, secret: undefined }. The first-run default at line 90 includes npmPlugins: []. Add npmPlugins: [] to FALLBACK_CONFIG. This is a one-line change. The fallback path at line 103 spreads FALLBACK_CONFIG, so it will automatically pick up the new field.

userStories.title: Clean up stale .tmp files in cleanupStaleExecFiles
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want stale .tmp exec files cleaned up on server startup so that crashed sessions don't leave orphan files.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanupStaleExecFiles at extension-protocol.ts:744-758 also deletes files matching EXEC_FILE_PREFIX + *.js.tmp
Filter logic matches both .js and .js.tmp suffixed files with the exec prefix
userStories.notes: extension-protocol.ts:753 — current filter is entries.filter(f => f.startsWith(EXEC_FILE_PREFIX) && f.endsWith('.js')). The writeExecFile function at line 726 creates .tmp files as intermediates (tmpPath = finalPath + '.tmp'). Change the filter to: entries.filter(f => f.startsWith(EXEC_FILE_PREFIX) && (f.endsWith('.js') || f.endsWith('.js.tmp'))). This handles crash leftovers where the rename never completed.

userStories.title: Eliminate redundant double notifyToolListChanged on hot reload
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want to receive only one tools/list_changed notification per reload so that I don't double-fetch the tool list.
acceptanceCriteria: During hot reload (performReload), each MCP client receives exactly one tools/list_changed notification, not two
During config reload (performConfigReload), MCP clients still receive a notification
The notification happens after handler re-registration in performReload (lines 295-297), not inside reloadCore
userStories.notes: reload.ts:176 calls notifyAllClients() inside reloadCore, and then performReload calls notifyToolListChanged again at lines 295-297. Remove the notifyAllClients() call from inside reloadCore (line 176). However, performConfigReload also calls reloadCore and relies on that notification since it does NOT call notifyToolListChanged separately. Solution: remove the call at line 176, and add a notifyAllClients call in performConfigReload after the reloadCore call (around line 348). The notifyAllClients helper is a local function inside reloadCore — you may need to extract it or inline the loop in performConfigReload.

userStories.title: Derive BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES dynamically from browserTools array
userStories.description: As a security-conscious platform, I want the prompt injection detection to cover ALL browser tools so that malicious plugins cannot reference unchecked sensitive tools.
acceptanceCriteria: BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in discovery.ts is derived from the browserTools array, not hardcoded
All 35+ browser tool names are included in the prompt injection check
The derivation is at module scope (computed once), not inside the check function
userStories.notes: discovery.ts:481-487 — BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES is a hardcoded array of 5 names. Import { browserTools } from './browser-tools/index.js' and derive: const BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES = browserTools.map(t => t.name). This ensures the list stays in sync automatically. The browserTools array is defined in browser-tools/index.ts and has 35 entries. Note: the names in browserTools use the format 'browser_*' (underscored), which matches the format used in the prompt injection check at line 499 (descLower comparison). Verify the import doesn't create a circular dependency — discovery.ts currently imports from shared and nothing from browser-tools.

userStories.title: Add at-least-one validation for select-option tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want a clear error when I invoke browser_select_option without providing value or label so that I don't get a confusing late error.
acceptanceCriteria: When neither value nor label is provided, the tool returns a clear error message before dispatching to the extension
The error message says 'At least one of value or label must be provided'
When value is provided alone, the tool works correctly
When label is provided alone, the tool works correctly
When both are provided, the tool works correctly
userStories.notes: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/select-option.ts:14-19 — both value and label are z.string().optional(). Add validation in the browser command handler (browser-commands.ts handleBrowserSelectOption function) to check that at least one of params.value or params.label is defined. If neither is provided, send an error response: { code: -32602, message: 'At least one of value or label must be provided' }. Do NOT use .refine() on the Zod schema — per CLAUDE.md, transforms/refines on input schemas can break JSON Schema serialization. The handler-level check is the correct pattern for this codebase.

userStories.title: Fix atob UTF-8 corruption in getResourceContent
userStories.description: As a user, I want base64-encoded text resources to be decoded correctly so that non-ASCII characters (Chinese, emoji) are not garbled.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserGetResourceContent uses TextDecoder for base64 text decoding instead of bare atob
The pattern matches network-capture.ts line 204-205: new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(content), c => c.charCodeAt(0)))
Non-ASCII characters in base64-encoded text resources are preserved correctly
userStories.notes: browser-commands.ts:1760 — current code is content = atob(content) which returns Latin-1. Replace with content = new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(content), c => c.charCodeAt(0))). This matches the existing correct pattern at network-capture.ts:204-205. The try-catch on line 1762 already handles decode failures, so keep the structure: try { content = new TextDecoder().decode(Uint8Array.from(atob(content), c => c.charCodeAt(0))); base64Encoded = false; } catch { /* return as-is */ }

userStories.title: Fix typeText to validate element is a text input before casting
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want typeText to return a clear error when targeting non-input elements so that I don't get silent false success.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserTypeText checks the element tagName before casting to HTMLInputElement
If the element is not input, textarea, or contenteditable, returns error: 'Element is not a text input (found <tagname>)'
Existing behavior for input/textarea elements is unchanged
userStories.notes: browser-commands.ts:524-526 — after const el = document.querySelector(sel), add a tag check before the cast. The correct pattern: const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase(); const isEditable = tag === 'input' || tag === 'textarea' || (el as HTMLElement).isContentEditable; if (!isEditable) return { error: 'Element ' + sel + ' is not a text input (found <' + tag + '>)' }; Then proceed with the existing cast and logic. This matches the same tag-check pattern used in handleBrowserPressKey at line 1257-1258.

userStories.title: Fix pressKey to mutate element value for printable characters
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want pressKey with a printable character to actually insert text into input elements so that the behavior matches what InputEvent implies.
acceptanceCriteria: For printable characters on input/textarea elements, pressKey inserts the character into element.value before dispatching InputEvent
For contentEditable elements, uses document.execCommand('insertText', false, key) or equivalent
The keyboard event sequence (keydown, keypress, keyup) is preserved
The InputEvent dispatch is preserved (after value mutation)
Non-printable keys (Enter, Tab, Escape, arrows) are NOT affected
userStories.notes: browser-commands.ts:1256-1269 — after the 'if (isPrintable)' check and before dispatching InputEvent, insert the character into the element value. For input/textarea: get current selectionStart/selectionEnd, splice the character in, set the new value, and update selection. For contentEditable: use document.execCommand('insertText', false, k). The typeText handler at lines 526-528 shows the simple case (replaces entire value), but pressKey should insert at cursor position. Pattern: const input = target as HTMLInputElement; const start = input.selectionStart ?? input.value.length; const end = input.selectionEnd ?? start; input.value = input.value.slice(0, start) + k + input.value.slice(end); input.selectionStart = input.selectionEnd = start + 1; Then dispatch the InputEvent.

userStories.title: Fix Slack token regex missing underscore in localStorage scan
userStories.description: As a Slack plugin user, I want token extraction from localStorage to correctly handle tokens with underscores so that authentication works reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Regex at slack-api.ts:248 uses [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ (with underscore) instead of [a-zA-Z0-9-]+
Matches the character class used at line 157
userStories.notes: plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts:248 — change /(xoxc-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)/ to /(xoxc-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/. This is a one-character fix. The regex at line 157 already includes the underscore: /["']?api_token["']?\s*:\s*["'](xoxc-[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)["']/. IMPORTANT: this is a plugin file, not a platform file. Build with: cd plugins/slack && bun run build. The root bun run build does NOT build plugins.

userStories.title: Wrap slackApi response.json() in try-catch for ToolError consistency
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want all errors from slackApi to be ToolError instances so that error handling is consistent and predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: response.json() call at slack-api.ts:410 is wrapped in try-catch
JSON parse failures throw ToolError('Failed to parse Slack API response', 'invalid_response')
Existing behavior for valid JSON responses is unchanged
userStories.notes: plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts:410 — change from const data: unknown = await response.json(); to: let data: unknown; try { data = await response.json(); } catch { throw new ToolError('Failed to parse Slack API response', 'invalid_response'); }. ToolError is already imported (line 1). IMPORTANT: this is a plugin file. Build with: cd plugins/slack && bun run build.

userStories.title: Extract withDebugger helper from duplicated debugger attach/detach pattern
userStories.description: As a developer, I want debugger attach/detach logic in browser-commands.ts to be a shared helper so that new debugger-using commands get the pattern for free and bugs are fixed in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: A withDebugger helper function exists in browser-commands.ts that handles: checking isCapturing, attaching debugger with error message for 'Another debugger', and detaching in finally block
handleBrowserHandleDialog uses withDebugger instead of inline attach/detach
handleBrowserListResources uses withDebugger instead of inline attach/detach
handleBrowserGetResourceContent uses withDebugger instead of inline attach/detach
Behavior is identical to the current inline pattern
userStories.notes: browser-commands.ts has three functions with near-identical debugger boilerplate at lines 1552-1601, 1623-1679, 1706-1783. Extract: async function withDebugger<T>(tabId: number, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> { const alreadyAttached = isCapturing(tabId); if (!alreadyAttached) { try { await chrome.debugger.attach({ tabId }, '1.3'); } catch (err) { const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); throw new Error(msg.includes('Another debugger') ? 'Failed to attach debugger — another debugger...' : 'Failed to attach debugger: ' + sanitizeErrorMessage(msg)); } } try { return await fn(); } finally { if (!alreadyAttached) { await chrome.debugger.detach({ tabId }).catch(() => {}); } } }. The caller catches the thrown Error and sends the jsonrpc error response.

userStories.title: Extract wrapAsyncHandler helper to eliminate message-router dispatch boilerplate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the message-router dispatch table to use a helper function so that adding new handlers is a one-liner and the id/catch pattern cannot be forgotten.
acceptanceCriteria: A wrapAsyncHandler helper function exists that wraps async handlers with the id !== undefined check and .catch(err => console.warn(...))
A wrapSyncHandler helper exists for sync handlers (no .catch needed)
All browser command entries in methodHandlers use the wrapper instead of inline lambdas
Behavior is identical to the current dispatch table
The known-methods.test.ts test continues to pass (it checks methodHandlerNames)
